results.title: Australia
results.creator_credit: Stafford Garner, Stewart MacLennan and National Geographic Society
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Families of the world”.
Join 11 year-old Catherine Inall and learn about her life in Sydney and her Christmas summer vacation on a sheep farm in remote northern New South Wales. Catherine participates in a way of life that is both new to her and unique to Australia. Designed for students from the Northern hemisphere, it is interesting to look at Australian life from a foreigner’s perspective. Suitable for Mid-Upper Primary level students. Teacher’s guide included.

results.title: Costa Rica
results.creator_credit: Horst Cerns, Rolando Quiros, Japanese TV and UNICEF
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Families of the world”.
Captures the flavour of life on a Costa Rican coffee plantation during harvest time when the whole family helps. At harvest’s end the family joins the village fiesta, parading by day and revelling by night.

results.title: Turkey
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Families of the world”.
Shows Turkish village life. Girls weave or embroider, and father and mother farm. At day’s end, the family gathers for the evening meal and later to answer the call to prayer. A trip to Instanbul offers a respite from the normal daily routine.

results.title: Cats
results.creator_credit: Alan Degen and National Geographic Society
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of pets”.
This video explores the life of a housecat, from the time of its birth, through its early months of development to adulthood. It explores a cats ability to always land on its feet, its natural agility and flexibility. Also explained are the cat’s mechanisms of perception and communication; the use of the tongue, specialised scent glands, ears and whiskers and its voice. Suitable for Mid-Upper Primary level students. Teachers guide included.

results.title: World of pets : Fish
results.creator_credit: Dennis B. Kane and National Geographic Society
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of pets”.
This film explores the world of fish by demonstrating to students how to set up an aquarium and how to care for goldfish, as well as a practical discussion of the anatomy, perception and breeding behaviour of fish. Students will discover that fish not only make colourful pets but that they also can be used to explore animal behaviour and to teach responsibility. Suitable for Middle-Upper Primary level students. Teacher’s guide included.

results.title: World of pets : dogs
results.creator_credit: Chris Brown and National Geographic Society
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “World of pets”.
This film explores the world of the dog as a companion to humans. By focussing on a golden retriever and her puppies, from birth to maturity, we observe the dog’s behaviour as it interacts with other dogs and the human family which adopts it. Suitable for Middle-Upper Primary level students. Teacher’s guide included.

results.title: From the earth to the moon
results.creator_credit: Brian Grazer, Michael Bostick, Ron Howard, Clavius Base and Imagine Entertainment (US)
results.brief_description: Produced with the cooperation of NASA, these are the stories of the men, women and children who lived and breathed the great American dream as it became reality through the voyages of the Apollo astronauts in their mission to place a man on the moon.

results.title: We interrupt this program
results.creator_credit: Brian Grazer, Michael Bostick, Ron Howard, David Frankel, Clavius Base and Imagine Entertainment (US)
results.brief_description:
Episode number 8 of Series “From the earth to the moon”.
On April 13, 1970, Apollo 13, the 23rd manned flight into space, developed problems that threatened the lives of the astronauts on board. The complacency which had accompanied the launch and the disinterest of the general public throughout the mission until problems occurred were quickly dispelled, as every move was monitored by journalists who fed the story to a hungry audience fast becoming hooked on sensationalism. A divide was growing wider between the traditions of the intellectual press and the fast-feed emotional content of the new tabloid journalism.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Brian Grazer, Michael Bostick, Ron Howard, David Frankel, Clavius Base and Imagine Entertainment (US)

results.title: Mare tranquilitatis
results.creator_credit: Brian Grazer, Michael Bostick, Ron Howard, Frank Marshall, Clavius Base and Imagine Entertainment (US)
results.brief_description:
Episode number 6 of Series “From the earth to the moon”.
It would be the culmination of John F. Kennedy’s dream. On July 20th 1969, after years of development and training, Apollo 11 - with its team of Neil Armstrong, Edward “Buzz” Aldrin Jr., and Michael Collins - is about to land a man on the moon. The world is watching, and America holds its breath as Neil Armstrong takes “one small step for man - one giant leap for mankind.”
results.creator_credit_for_label: Brian Grazer, Michael Bostick, Ron Howard, Frank Marshall, Clavius Base and Imagine Entertainment (US)
